data.body:
## Bug Description

When merge-to-main.sh runs with --bump minor and the version_bump phase's commit fails (e.g., test gate blocks it), the state file records version_bump as incomplete. On --resume, the script re-enters version_bump and bumps the version AGAIN (0.31.1 → 0.32.0 → 0.33.0), creating a double-bump loop.

## Root Cause

The version_bump phase calls bump-version.sh which modifies plugin.json, then tries to commit. If the commit fails (test gate, review gate), the phase is not marked complete in the state file. On resume, bump-version.sh runs again and increments from the already-bumped value.

## Expected Behavior

The version_bump phase should be idempotent — if the version was already bumped but not committed, --resume should commit the existing bump, not bump again.

## Reproduction

1. Run merge-to-main.sh --bump minor
2. Let it bump the version but fail at the commit step (e.g., test gate blocks)
3. Run merge-to-main.sh --bump minor --resume
4. Observe the version bumps again (double-bump)

## Suggested Fix

Before bumping, check if the current version already reflects a bump (compare against the version on main). If the version is already ahead of main, skip the bump and proceed to commit.
